sections.paragraph:
pl. מְצָדֹות with Kametz impure (from the root צוּד to hunt, to lie in wait, like מְעָרָה from the root עוּר), pr. a place whence hunters seek their prey, and to which they can flee as into a safe retreat. Hence-

(1) the top, the summit of a mountain, difficult of access (Arab. مَطَدُ, which some incorrectly refer to the root مصد), 1Sa 23:14, 19 1Sa 23:19 1Ch 12:8, 16 1Ch 12:16compare Jud. 6:2 Eze 33:27 see also מָצֹוד, מְצֹודָה, מְצוּדָה.

(2) a fortress, a mountain castle. 1Ch 11:7, וַיֵשֶׁב דָּוִיד בִּמְצָד “David dwelt in the fortress (of Zion).” Jer 48:41 51:30 .
